report_name: Akira Ransomware Claims Attack on Marketing Firm Ironmark

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that emerged in early 2023, known for its retro-themed dark web leak site. The group claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Ironmark, employing a double-extortion strategy by exfiltrating 190GB of data and threatening its public release.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victims through data encryption and the threat of public data leaks.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Akira ransomware was deployed to encrypt files on Ironmark's network as part of a double-extortion attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The Akira threat actors exfiltrated 190GB of sensitive data, including employee PII and corporate financial records, from Ironmark's network to an external location to pressure the victim into paying the ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Akira operators are known to use vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery from snapshots.

malware.name: Akira
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Akira group to encrypt victim files. In the attack against Ironmark, it was used to encrypt network files and typically appends the .akira extension.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: A credential dumping tool commonly used by the Akira group to harvest credentials from memory after gaining initial access.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate remote administration tool frequently used by Akira operators for lateral movement across a compromised network.

tools.name: FileZilla
tools.description: An FTP client tool mentioned as being used by Akira for data exfiltration prior to encryption.

tools.name: WinSCP
tools.description: An SFTP/FTP client tool mentioned as being used by Akira for data exfiltration.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool observed in use by Akira operators.

tools.name: RustDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool observed in use by Akira operators.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: A Windows command-line utility used by Akira operators to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: Ironmark
identities.description: A U.S.-based marketing and communications services firm targeted by the Akira ransomware group. The attackers claimed to have stolen 190GB of data, including employee PII, financial records, and client contracts.

infrastructure.name: Akira Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The dark web site used by the Akira ransomware group to publish stolen data from victims who do not pay the ransom. Ironmark was added to this site on July 13, 2026.

indicators.name: Akira Ransomware File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Ransomware File Extension Detection
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b2a-9c1d-0e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the '.akira' extension, which is characteristic of the Akira ransomware.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/akira-ransomware-targets-ironmark/
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.akira'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the .akira extension, a definitive sign of a successful Akira ransomware attack.

indicators.name: Akira Associated Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Associated Tool Execution
id: f4d3e2c1-b0a9-4f8e-8d7c-6b5a4f3e2d1c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for the execution of tools commonly used by the Akira ransomware group for credential access and remote control.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/akira-ransomware-targets-ironmark/
level: high
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mimikatz.exe'
      - '\psexec.exe'
      - '\anydesk.exe'
      - '\rustdesk.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools like Mimikatz, PsExec, AnyDesk, or RustDesk, which are commonly used by Akira operators during their attacks.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/akira-ransomware-targets-ironmark/
level: high
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used by Akira and other ransomware groups to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery.
